items.title: Abu Dhabi Royal Family Firm to Invest $1.13 Billion in LNG Company MidOcean
items.description: An investment firm owned by members of Abu Dhabi’s ruling family will invest $1.13 billion in liquefied natural gas company MidOcean Energy LLC.

items.title: BP Drove Up European Jet Fuel Output by 30% During Iran Conflict
items.description: BP Plc boosted jet fuel production at its European refineries by about 30% during the Iran conflict, helping the region avoid major shortages while millions of barrels were locked up in the Persian Gu

items.title: Santé Cie Loan Price Jumps as Firm Plans Lender Negotiations
items.description: Traders are rapidly marking up prices for Santé Cie SAS’s loan after the home healthcare provider said it was looking to begin an out-of-court process to negotiate with its lenders.

items.title: German Oil Stocking Agency Says It’s Refilling Strategic Reserve
items.description: Germany’s oil stockpiling agency EBV is looking to refill strategic reserves of diesel, a move that could pile more pressure on an already tight fuel market.

items.title: Apple Supplier Luxshare Prices $3.1 Billion HK Listing at Top
items.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. raised HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) after pricing shares for its Hong Kong listing at the maximum amount it had set.

items.title: Russian Oil Flows Hit New High But Slumping Prices Crush Income
items.description: Urals prices back where they were before US, Israel launched campaign against Iran

items.title: Dangote Says Kenya Refinery Will Cost as Much as $17 Billion
items.description: A new mega-refinery to be built at the Kenyan coast by Africa’s richest person will cost as much as $17 billion, a spokesman for Dangote Industries Ltd. has confirmed.

items.title: InsuranceDekho Said to Pick Banks for $400 Million India IPO
items.description: Indian insurance aggregator InsuranceDekho’s parent has selected HSBC Holdings Plc, Morgan Stanley, ICICI Securities Ltd. and IIFL Capital Services Ltd. as advisers to a potential initial public offer

items.title: US Trade Deficit Widens to Biggest in More Than a Year
items.description: The US trade deficit widened in May as a broad-based increase in imports and a decline in exports propelled the gap to the most in more than a year.

items.title: France Unveils New Cuts as Weaker Growth Imperils Deficit Goal
items.description: France said it would make €3 billion ($3.4 billion) of additional budget cuts this year as it struggles to keep on track with narrowing the budget shortfall after weaker economic activity threatened i

items.title: Equinor’s Chief Crude Trader Leaves After Nine-Year Tenure
items.description: Equinor ASA’s chief crude trader, David Barratt, left the company this week following a string of other departures from the Norwegian energy giant.

items.title: Standard Nuclear Seeks to Raise $383 Million in US IPO
items.description: Standard Nuclear Inc., a maker of fuel for nuclear reactors, is seeking to raise as much as $383 million in a US initial public offering.

items.title: Gas Demand Set for First Drop Since 2022 on Iran War, IEA Says
items.description: Global gas demand is poised for the first annual drop since the 2022 energy crisis because of higher prices caused by the Iran war, according to the International Energy Agency.

items.title: Autodoc Is Raising €530 Million Loan to Buy Out Apollo’s Stake
items.description: German online car-parts retailer Autodoc Holding SE is raising €530 million ($606 million) in debt financing to help it buy back a minority stake held by Apollo Global Management Inc.

items.title: Amazon Seeks to Raise at Least $25 Billion From Bond Sale
items.description: Amazon.com Inc. is looking to raise at least $25 billion from a US dollar bond sale, its latest funding push as the company ramps up investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure.

items.title: Accounting Firm CBIZ Urged by Activist Investor to Pursue M&A
items.description: A CBIZ Inc. shareholder is urging the accounting firm to re-evaluate its capital allocation, abandon a share buyback plan and return to pursuing acquisitions, according to a letter sent to the company

items.title: Dudley: Still Bullish on AI Spending
items.description: Katrina Dudley, Franklin Templeton Senior Investment Strategist, discusses the slump in Samsung shares and why she continues to be bullish on AI infrastructure spending. She speaks with Bloomberg's Vo

items.title: Samsung Earnings Send Chip Stocks Tumbling as Kospi Triggers Circuit Breaker | The Pulse 7/7/2026
items.description: "The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story

items.title: Trump Arrives in Turkey With Iran War at Top of Agenda
items.description: President Donald Trump lands in Ankara, Turkey for the NATO summit where he will start the day visiting with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern is on the ground with

items.title: Tech Volatility Hits Highest Since Dot-Com Bust Next to S&P 500
items.description: The higher the rally in technology high-flyers, the louder the anxiety around a new wave of turbulence in the group.
